originalAlarm.description: Branch condition evaluates to a garbage value

sanitizedDescription: Branch condition evaluates to a garbage value
presenceCondition:
(or (and (not CONFIG_FEATURE_HTTPD_CGI)

         CONFIG_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH)
    (and CONFIG_FEATURE_HTTPD_CGI

  false)
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool

(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH () Bool
  true)
reasoning: 5cd6461b6fb.c L30: `if (http_unauthorized)` — branch condition allegedly garbage. However, http_unauthorized is assigned at L28 (`int http_unauthorized = random;`) and random is assigned at L27 (`int random = rand() % 2;`), all inside the same #ifdef CONFIG_FEATURE_HTTPD_BASIC_AUTH block. The value IS initialized before use at L30 within the same BASIC_AUTH block. False positive — CSA may find a desugared path where the BASIC_AUTH runtime variable is treated inconsistently. PC simplifies to BASIC_AUTH=T (plus library macros that cancel out), but the alarm itself is a false positive.
